condition_specific: mesothelioma, testicular cancer, intestinal cancer, esophageal cancer
notes: This is a line-of-duty DEATH BENEFIT presumption only (Public Safety Employees' Death Benefits Act), not a workers' compensation presumption. Death from one of the four listed cancers is presumed to be occupationally related to firefighting and therefore a line-of-duty death, triggering a $100,000 death benefit to dependents. Cancers must be 'occupationally related to firefighting.' No rebuttability provision stated.

condition_specific: malignant neoplasms caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or a known carcinogen as defined by WHO/IARC
statute_citation: N.C. Gen. Stat. §§ 58-86A-1 through 58-86A-6 (Firefighters' Cancer Insurance Program)
filing_deadline_notes: Must have a new diagnosis of cancer on or after January 1, 2022; prior diagnosed cancer type ineligible, but new cancer types diagnosed on or after Jan 1, 2022 remain eligible even if metastasized from earlier cancer
notes: This is a supplemental state insurance benefits program, NOT a workers' compensation presumption. Benefits are supplemental to other health benefits. Firefighter is ineligible if simultaneously receiving WC benefits under Chapter 97 for the same cancer. Eligibility: 5 continuous years of service in an NC fire department (lapse of up to 6 months does not break continuity), OR on NC State Firefighters' Association certified roster as 'retired/nonactive' for no more than 10 years post-service. Benefits: lump sum up to $74,000 total ($37,000 per diagnosis); disability benefit of 75% of monthly salary or $5,000/month max (whichever less) for non-volunteers, or $1,500/month for volunteers, beginning 6 months after total disability, for up to 36 consecutive months. Disability benefit subordinate to other disability benefits from other sources (except private insurance purchased solely by the firefighter). Volunteer firefighters explicitly covered. Administered by the Office of the State Fire Marshal via a contracted third-party administrator.

condition_specific: malignant neoplasms caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or a known carcinogen as defined by WHO/IARC
statute_citation: N.C. Gen. Stat. §§ 58-86A-1 through 58-86A-6 (Firefighters' Cancer Insurance Program)
filing_deadline_notes: Must have a new diagnosis of cancer on or after January 1, 2022
notes: Volunteer firefighters explicitly included in the Firefighters' Cancer Insurance Program (§ 58-86A-4(2)(b)). Disability benefit for volunteers is $1,500/month (not salary-based) for up to 36 consecutive months beginning 6 months after total disability. Lump-sum benefit structure same as non-volunteers ($37,000 per diagnosis, max $74,000 total). Same eligibility requirements apply (5 continuous years of service). Ineligible if simultaneously receiving WC benefits for the same cancer.
